description: Revised backlog seed manifest after Phase 2 audit normalization. Design work moved to repo docs (Phase 4). Only implementation/content/tooling work becomes GitHub issues (Phase 5). Secret meta-narrative integrated as sub-track.
design_note: Groups 01-09 from the original manifest have been reclassified as Phase 3-4 design work (repo docs, not issues). This manifest now only contains issue-eligible groups for Phase 5.

groups.title: M1: First Playable Store — Implementation
groups.description: Core implementation issues for M1 vertical slice. One playable sports card store with buy/sell loop, one customer type, day cycle, and HUD.
items: Create ItemInstance class and integrate with InventorySystem
Wire DataLoader to parse all content JSON on boot
Implement player controller (WASD, mouse look, gravity, floor snap)
Implement interaction raycast and Press E prompt
Create sports store interior scene (shelves, counter, door, placeholders)
Implement shelf interaction — opens item placement UI
Implement inventory panel (grid, backroom items)
Implement item placement (backroom to shelf)
Implement one customer (walk in, browse, pick item, go to register)
Implement purchase flow (customer presents item, confirm price, money transfer)
Wire TimeSystem day cycle (hour advance, day end signals)
Implement HUD (cash, time, interaction prompt)
Implement end-of-day summary screen
Create starter sports card content (15-20 items in JSON)
Implement basic price setting UI
validation_requirements: Every issue maps to a MILESTONES.md M1 exit criterion
No issue assumes code that doesn't exist yet
Dependencies form a valid DAG
Acceptance criteria are testable
All issues reference actual file paths

groups.title: Content Pipeline Tooling
groups.description: Build validation and generation tools needed before bulk content authoring.
items: JSON schema validation script (validates content against DATA_MODEL.md)
Duplicate ID detection across all content files
Content statistics reporter (counts per store/rarity/category)
Content generation templates (one per store type)
Add content validation to GitHub Actions CI
validation_requirements: Validation catches missing required fields
Validation catches duplicate IDs
Templates match finalized schema
CI runs validation on every PR

groups.title: M2: Economy and Customer AI — Implementation
groups.description: Issues for M2: multiple customer types, pathfinding, haggling, dynamic pricing, operating costs, reputation, save/load, audio.
items: Multiple customer profiles (collector, casual, bargain hunter, kid)
Customer pathfinding via NavigationServer3D
Haggling mechanic implementation
Dynamic pricing (market value shifts with supply/demand)
Operating costs (rent, utilities deducted per day)
Stock ordering UI (browse catalog, pay, delivery next day)
Reputation system wiring (score changes from transactions)
Save/load implementation (serialize/deserialize all systems)
Settings menu (volume, display, controls)
Sound effects (purchase, door, ambient)
Background music system
validation_requirements: Issues map to MILESTONES.md M2 exit criteria
Customer types reference CUSTOMER_AI.md specification
Economy issues reference ECONOMY_BALANCE.md targets

groups.title: M3: Multiple Stores and Build Mode — Implementation
groups.description: Issues for M3: second and third store types, build mode, mall navigation, trends.
items: Retro game store implementation (scene, controller, unique mechanic)
Video rental store implementation (scene, controller, rental mechanic)
Store unlock system (reputation + funds gate)
Build mode (grid overlay, fixture placement, move/rotate)
Upgrade system (better fixtures, signage per store)
Mall navigation (multiple storefronts, common spaces)
Trend system (hot/cold items, demand shifts)
PocketCreatures store implementation
Electronics store implementation
More customer variety (groups, store-type preferences)
Content: item sets for retro games, video rental, PocketCreatures, electronics
validation_requirements: Each store references its deep-dive design doc
Store implementations extend StoreController without modifying core systems
Build mode is scoped to placement only (no complex grid editing)

groups.title: M4: Progression, Events, Polish — Implementation
groups.description: Issues for M4: campaign structure, milestones, events, staff, polish, tutorial, accessibility.
items: Campaign structure (start with one store, grow to mall mogul)
Milestone/unlock system (rewards at reputation/revenue thresholds)
Seasonal events (holiday rushes, back-to-school, summer slump)
Random events (supply shortage, viral trend, health inspection)
Staff hiring system (employees auto-manage stores imperfectly)
Tutorial / onboarding flow
Visual polish pass
UI polish pass (transitions, feedback animations)
Accessibility (key rebinding, UI scaling, colorblind)
Performance profiling and optimization
validation_requirements: Progression issues reference PROGRESSION.md unlock sequence
Event issues reference EVENTS_AND_TRENDS.md taxonomy
Staff system has a design doc before implementation begins

groups.title: M5-M7: Remaining Milestones
groups.description: Issues for later milestones. Generated after M1-M4 backlog is stable.
items: M5: Validate modular architecture with second store type; store-specific mechanic polish
M6: Supplier tier progression; item appreciation/depreciation over time; ordering system
M7: Art pass; save/load QA; export testing; store page assets
validation_requirements: M5 proves no core system changes needed for new store types
M7 covers all release-readiness criteria

groups.title: Secret Meta-Narrative — Implementation Sub-Track
groups.description: Hidden thread implementation. Small, scoped, layered on top of existing systems. Never on critical path.
items: Hidden state tracking system (secret_state in save data)
Clue content authoring (15-25 clue definitions across categories)
Clue delivery hooks (integrate into events, customers, inventory)
Thread phase escalation logic (dormant → seeded → active → escalated)
Ending branch logic (score-based ending selection at 100%)
Three ending screens (normal, questioned, raid)
QA: thread isolation verification
QA: ending path testing
validation_requirements: Thread engagement does not affect core game metrics
All 3 endings are reachable
No issue is on the critical path for any milestone
All issues extend existing systems rather than creating new ones
Clue content uses existing content pipeline
groups.label: secret-thread

summary: 7 issue groups across 3 waves. Design work stays as repo docs in Phase 4. Only implementation becomes issues in Phase 5. Secret thread is 7 issues nested in M4-M6. Total estimate tightened from 92 to 90-97.
